body_txt: A lot of dragons in fiction are lazy creatures who don't do much apart from guarding their hoards . This typically translates into a sleeping dragon who has been slumbering for millennia, curled atop a pile of treasure in some deep and distant corner of the land ( although he will probably get woken up during the plot's unfolding, and that's probably bad news ). Their long (sometimes millennia-long) sleep may be handwaved as dragons being so long-lived that a century-long nap is a reasonable hibernation time for them . In some settings, this will be linked to the dragons' age, with younger specimens being more active in the world but spending longer and longer periods of time sleeping or idling in their lairs as they age. In particularly extreme examples, slumbering dragons might become covered in plants and soils and become indistinguishable from the landscape ... at least until they wake up. On the other hand, more comedic examples will take full advantage of the comedic potential of these gigantic beasts, acting like grouchy old men who never want to go out. May be based on the Truth in Television that snakes (on whom the earlier European dragons were based) and, indeed, most reptiles, are prone to lying down for hours on end to conserve energy or to warm themselves up. Many reptiles are also ambush predators, lying still so that prey doesn't see them. In fact, crocodilians may be the closest thing to real-life Lazy Dragons due to this. Catlike Dragons are also prone to this portrayal, since Cats Are Lazy . Naturally a subtrope of Lazy Reptile and Our Dragons Are Different . Often combined with Orcus on His Throne , All-Powerful Bystander and/or The Gods Must Be Lazy , depending on the power level, intelligence, and general monstrosity and morality of dragons in the setting. On the other hand, very rarely related to Dragon Their Feet , despite the names' similarities. May be combined with Cats Are Lazy for Catlike Dragons . See also Dumb Dinos for similar connotations regarding Dinosaurs Are Dragons . Compare Sleepy Enemy .
